---
title: "surface_boundary_map"
generated: true
description: "Per-vertex functional-connectivity boundary map."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.graph.parcellation`](../nitrix-graph-parcellation.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:surface_boundary_map -->
```python
surface_boundary_map(connectivity_profiles: Float[Array, 'n_vertices d_profile'], adjacency: ELL, *, similarity: Literal['eta_squared', 'pearson'] = 'eta_squared', aggregate: Literal['mean', 'max'] = 'mean', roi: Optional[Bool[Array, 'n_vertices']] = None) -> Float[Array, 'n_vertices']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:surface_boundary_map -->
Per-vertex functional-connectivity boundary map.

For each vertex, the dissimilarity $1 -
\operatorname{sim}(\mathrm{profile}_i, \mathrm{profile}_j)$ to each mesh
neighbour $j$ is aggregated into a scalar boundary value: high where
adjacent connectivity profiles differ (a putative areal border), low inside
a homogeneous region.  Composes [`semiring_ell_edge_aggregate`](../nitrix-semiring-ell_edge/semiring_ell_edge_aggregate.qmd) over
the `adjacency` ELL -- no new kernel -- so it inherits the
format-agnostic ELL substrate and is differentiable with respect to the
profiles.  The minima of the resulting map seed [`mesh_watershed`](../nitrix-graph-parcellation/mesh_watershed.qmd).

Connectivity profiles are typically the vertex-wise correlation of the
`(n_vertices, T)` vertex time-series (so `d_profile = n_vertices`); at
ico7 that profile is 163 842-dimensional per vertex, so it is generally
materialised or tiled via a map over the row axis at the call site (a
memory caveat, not part of the API here).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:surface_boundary_map -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `connectivity_profiles` | `Float` | `n_vertices` × `d_profile` | — | `(n_vertices, d_profile)` per-vertex profile (e.g. a connectivity fingerprint). |
| `adjacency` | `ELL` | — | — | Mesh adjacency in ELL format (e.g. from [`mesh_k_ring_adjacency`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/mesh_k_ring_adjacency.qmd)); real edges carry nonzero `values` and padding carries `0` (the standard ELL convention), which is how padding is masked out. |
| `similarity` | `Literal['eta_squared', 'pearson']` | — | `'eta_squared'` | `'eta_squared'` (default, the measure of Cohen et al., 2008; see [`eta_squared`](../nitrix-graph-parcellation/eta_squared.qmd)) or `'pearson'`. |
| `aggregate` | `Literal['mean', 'max']` | — | `'mean'` | `'mean'` (default; mean dissimilarity over neighbours) or `'max'` (the strongest neighbour border). |
| `roi` | `Optional[Bool[Array, 'n_vertices']]` | — | `None` | Optional `(n_vertices,)` boolean cortex mask. Edges to out-of-ROI neighbours (e.g. the medial wall, whose profiles are meaningless) are dropped so they raise no spurious boundary, and out-of-ROI vertices are returned as `0`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `n_vertices`. Per-vertex boundary map: the aggregated neighbour dissimilarity at each vertex, or `0` at out-of-ROI vertices when `roi` is given.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:surface_boundary_map -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_vertices` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d_profile` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
